articles.title: 800% returns in five years! Multibagger stock jumps 4% despite weak trends on Dalal Street
articles.summary: Rathi Steel & Power share price has fallen over 8% in one month and has declined 28% on a YTD basis. The smallcap stock has dropped 34% in one year and has plunged over 62% in two years. However, Rathi Steel share price has delivered multibagger returns of 831% in five years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Fractal Analytics: an AI leader, or expensive IT bet?
articles.summary: While its proprietary AI products and sticky client base set it apart from traditional IT firms, a hefty 40x valuation and high client concentration leave little room for error.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Vinay Rajani of HDFC Sec suggests Viyash Scientific, Federal Bank shares to buy in near-term
articles.summary: The Indian stock market saw heavy losses, with the BSE Sensex dropping 1.11% and Nifty 50 declining 1.22%, driven by geopolitical tensions and a global tech selloff. Rising crude oil prices raised inflation concerns, but broader indices displayed relative resilience.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Penny stock under ₹10 hits upper circuit despite stock market sell-off
articles.summary: Penny stock under ₹10: Kshitij Polyline share price has gained over 50% in one month, and has jumped more than 160% in three months. The penny stock has surged 141% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis, while it has rallied 84% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Carlsberg plans to file for $700-million India IPO in June, listing likely in 2026
articles.summary: Discussions for a potential Carlsberg India IPO come at a time when Absolut and Chivas Regal maker Pernod Ricard too is exploring a domestic listing.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: TCS, Wipro to Infosys: IT stocks bleed on AI and tech stocks selloff in global markets; Nifty IT dips 2%
articles.summary: The Nifty IT Index decreased by 2% on June 8, primarily due to significant losses in major stocks, particularly Wipro. Conversely, Tech Mahindra and a few others showed gains, helping to soften the index's drop.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Adani Ports shares in focus as company bags 10-year marine services contract for Argentina's first LNG export project
articles.summary: On a one-year basis, the Adani group stock is up 24%, and for three years, it has surged 148%. On a longer time frame of 10 years, Adani Ports share price has surged 780%.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Adani Power to Adani Green: Jefferies bullish on these Adani Group stocks. Should you buy?
articles.summary: Adani group achieved a record capex of ₹1.55 lakh crore in FY26 and remains confident of sustaining growth with a disciplined financial structure, Jefferies noted.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sun Pharma, Alkem Labs, other pharma stocks gain despite stock market crash; Nifty Pharma rises for fourth day in a row
articles.summary: The Nifty Pharma index rose 1.4% on 8 June, defying the broader market selloff, supported by heavyweight stocks like JB Chemicals and Alkem Laboratories. Despite some losses in others, analysts suggest pharma stocks are resilient during market instability due to consistent healthcare demand.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Vahh Chemicals IPO Day 3: GMP signals 15% listing pop. Check subscription, price band and other details
articles.summary: Vahh Chemicals, specialising in textile chemicals, has seen strong demand for its IPO, which closes today. With a subscription rate of over 16 times so far and a grey market premium, the company is set for a promising listing scheduled for June 11.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold Extends Loss as Israel-Iran Clashes Endanger Mideast Truce
articles.summary: Gold extended a decline after Israel said it struck Iranian targets in response to missile attacks by Tehran, threatening a fragile ceasefire in the Middle East.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: SMR Jewels share price gains after listing with 20% discount on BSE SME
articles.summary: SMR Jewels shares were listed at ₹102.95 apiece on BSE SME, a discount of 19.57% to the issue price of ₹128 per share. The stock dropped to an intraday low of ₹102.40 apiece.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Merritronix share price hits in 5% upper circuit after a blockbuster debut
articles.summary: Merritronix shares made a strong debut on BSE SME, opening at ₹283.10, 90% above its issue price of ₹149. The IPO attracted massive interest, with a subscription rate of 315.36 times, and the stock hit an upper circuit of ₹297.25.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold, silver rates today: MCX gold, silver prices dip on renewed inflation concern, US-Iran war in focus
articles.summary: Gold rate today: Following escalation in the US-Iran war, rising crude oil prices have put other assets under pressure, which includes gold and silver
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Treasuries Drop as Jobs Data, Iran Tensions Fuel Rate Hike Bets
articles.summary: Treasuries fell as investors ramped up bets that the Federal Reserve will need to raise interest rates, while escalating tensions in the Middle East added to inflation concerns.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stock market today: Gift Nifty hints gap-down start; US-Iran news to gold, silver rates — eight stocks to buy or sell
articles.summary: Stock market today: Experts recommend buying these eight shares today — Tata Technologies, Sai Life Sciences, Axis Bank, Eternal, Tata Steel, VA Tech Wabag, Diamond Power Infra, and Groww
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock market crash: Sensex recovers over 500 points from day’s low, Nifty 50 still below 23,300 - 5 key reasons
articles.summary: Stock market crash: Among sectors, Nifty IT, Nifty Metals, Nifty Auto and Nifty Realty slumped over 1.5% each, while Nifty Media and Nifty Pharma witnessed gains.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee opens 37 paise lower at 95.32 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell to 95.32 against the US dollar due to geopolitical tensions, rising oil prices, and a global selloff. RBI measures may attract $30-50 billion in capital inflows, improving outlook, though the currency remains sensitive to oil price movements.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO Day 2: Issue subscribed 2.63x so far. Check GMP, issue details, key dates. Apply or not?
articles.summary: The Hexagon Nutrition IPO opened with robust demand, fully subscribed within hours. The public issue runs from June 5-9, 2026. Valued at ₹138.87 crore, it consists of an offer-for-sale of 3.08 crore shares, with brokerages advising a 'Subscribe' rating.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO allotment date likely today. GMP, steps to check share allotment status online
articles.summary: CMR Green Technologies IPO allotment date is likely today, June 8, CMR Green Technologies IPO allotment status online check can be done through the websites of BSE and NSE, and also on the official portal of the IPO registrar. Kfin Technologies is the CMR Green Technologies IPO registrar.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to watch: Adani Energy, Hindustan Zinc, Lenskart, ZEE, Alembic Pharma, among key stocks in focus on 8 June
articles.summary: Indian stocks are expected to open lower following a global market selloff influenced by geopolitical issues. Key market activities include substantial investments by SBI Mutual Fund in Adani companies and various strategic moves by other firms in the pharmaceutical sector.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Buy or sell: Gift Nifty dips, Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — 8 June 2026
articles.summary: Buy or sell: Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — Schneider Electric Infrastructure, Axis Bank, and INOX Green
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 8 June
articles.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a gap-down start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 23,118 level, a discount of nearly 334 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Crude oil prices jump after Israel strikes on Lebanon; WTI rallies 4%, Brent oil above $96 a barrel
articles.summary: Crude oil prices jumped as hopes for an end to the wider US-Iran war and a restart to crude flows through the Strait of Hormuz eroded.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Anand Rathi's Jigar Patel suggests strategies for 3 shares
articles.summary: In a week marked by volatile markets and selling pressure, Jigar Patel from Anand Rathi shares trading strategies for three stocks, highlighting critical support levels and resistance zones.

articles.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today — 8 June 2026
articles.summary: Sensex and Nifty 50 are likely to open on a weak note on Monday, tracking a sharp selloff in global markets amid escalating tensions in the Middle East and fading hopes of a near-term resolution to the broader US-Iran conflict.

articles.title: Gift Nifty, Iran war, oil prices to Nasdaq, Nikkei’s crash: 10 things that changed for Indian stock market over weekend
articles.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 23,096 level, a discount of nearly 356 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a gap-down start for the Indian stock market indices.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Asian markets crash: Nikkei slips over 3%, Kopsi tanks 8% amid escalating US-Iran war, rising crude oil prices
articles.summary: Asian markets crash: Asian markets fell sharply on 8 June as investors took profits in tech stocks, with South Korea's KOSPI dropping over 6.8%. Geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns added pressure, following a significant decline in US markets.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Korean Stocks Tumble as Investors Rush to Offload Tech Shares
articles.summary: South Korean stocks dropped, led by steep losses in chipmaker shares on an intensifying rotation out of artificial intelligence beneficiaries.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stock recommendations for 8 June from MarketSmith India
articles.summary: MarketSmith India reveals its top stock recommendations for today, 8 June. Get expert insights into the best-performing stocks to guide your investment decisions.

articles.title: Another year of earnings disappointment? FY27 risks are mounting
articles.summary: War-driven inflation, slowing demand and lofty market valuations are raising doubts over whether corporate earnings can deliver the growth investors expect in FY27.

articles.title: FPIs will return when global macro conditions stabilise: Franklin Templeton India president
articles.summary: Despite  margin pressures over the next few quarters, cooling large cap valuations have improved the  risk-reward ratio for investors over the medium term, according to Avinash Satwalekar, president of Franklin Templeton - India.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Japan’s Stocks Set to Sink on Tech Selloff, Middle East Worries
articles.summary: Japanese stocks are set to fall, led by tech shares, after strong US jobs data fueled expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep rates higher for longer, sparking a selloff in AI-related shares on Wall Street.

articles.title: How the government hit its FY26 deficit target despite a subsidy surge
articles.summary: Higher subsidies and lower receipts complicated the Centre's fiscal math in FY26. Yet, it met its deficit target for FY26. Mint breaks down the math.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude price fears won't derail economy: PM-EAC's Neelkanth Mishra says India can grow over 8% amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: India's economic growth remains resilient despite rising crude oil prices, as per Neelkanth Mishra of the PM-EAC. He highlights India's strong position to manage oil costs and predicts growth over 8% by early 2026.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s milk output to grow 6% in FY27 despite weak monsoon risks
articles.summary: Higher milk output is expected on the back of an increase in livestock population, better breeding, veterinary care, and expansion of dairy infrastructure through the addition of nearly 75,000 new dairy cooperatives.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt is changing the way you buy cooking oil — standard pack sizes will now help shoppers compare prices
articles.summary: The order targets a common pricing tactic in the world's most populous nation, where oils sold in odd, non-standard sizes leave buyers unable to tell which brand offers the best value for the kitchen staple.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The move into gold by other nations has happened before. It’s bad news for US dollar
articles.summary: The recent shift from U.S. assets to gold mirrors 1971, when central banks dumped dollars before President Nixon ended gold convertibility.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The week in charts: deficit concerns, IIP reset, India’s wedding dreams
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Solar power curtailment raises red flags for renewable investors
articles.summary: The solar capacity that was curtailed on 1 May equals almost one-sixth of the country’s installed capacity of 150GW. Experts said that low demand due to industries closing for Labour Day on 1 May may have contributed to the curtailment.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India targets near fourfold jump in fisheries exports to $30 billion in 5 years
articles.summary: Commerce minister Piyush Goyal noted that seafood exports rose from around $5 billion in 2013-14 to about $8.5 billion in FY26, while global fisheries trade increased from $150 billion to $164 billion during the same period.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India posts 7.7% GDP growth in FY26, lifted by strong March quarter
articles.summary: Despite the quarterly moderation, the annual figure came in slightly above market expectations for a 7.6% expansion, cushioned by a stronger-than-anticipated January-March performance. The economy had grown 7.1% in 2024-25.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: ‘So what? 100 is just a number’: PM Modi's EAC member dismisses alarm over rupee’s potential fall to triple digits
articles.summary: Shamika Ravi, a member of the PM's Economic Advisory Council, downplays concerns about the Indian rupee reaching 100 against USD, stating it's just a number. She emphasised managing the economic impact of global supply shocks over exchange rate defence.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India GDP Highlights: Q4FY26 GDP at 7.8%, real GDP growth at 7.7%, says govt
articles.summary: India GDP Highlights: MoSPI releases Q4 FY26 and annual GDP data shortly. Track growth rate, sector performance, comparison with estimates, RBI outlook, and market reaction.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI, govt unveil measures to attract foreign inflows in bonds, equities
articles.summary: The RBI and government delivered on a coordinated effort towards shoring up capital flows into Indian assets to ease the pressure on the rupee, experts said
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI MPC outcome: Central bank slashes FY27 growth projections to 6.6% amid Middle East crisis
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in its June policy meeting on Friday lowered its growth projections for the financial year 2026-27 (FY27) to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier amid the Middle East crisis and elevated crude oil prices along with disruptions to the Southwest monsoon.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: War and weather force RBI into a wait-and-watch mode as risks to growth, inflation loom
articles.summary: RBI has trimmed its real GDP growth forecast for FY27 to 6.6% from 6.9% earlier this year, while raising its retail inflation outlook to 5.1% from 4.6%.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Oil rises as Hezbollah rejects ceasefire, raising fresh fears over West Asia peace
articles.summary: Oil prices rose after Hezbollah rejected a US-brokered Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, reviving concerns over wider regional tensions and their impact on global energy supplies.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Centre plans to allow PE firms, sovereign wealth funds to directly participate in all greenfield infra projects
articles.summary: The Centre is planning to introduce a new public-private partnership framework to allow PE firms, pension funds, sovereign wealth funds and other large financial investors to directly participate in greenfield project development across sectors like power, railways, airports.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Govt nears a quarter of ₹80,000-cr asset-sale target for FY27
articles.summary: The government has raised ₹6,366.93 crore through land monetisation via Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs), while stake sales in Central Bank of India and Coal India fetched another ₹7,808.49 crore.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India remains among fastest-growing economies despite global uncertainty: Minister Prasada
articles.summary: The minister highlighted India's push in trade, manufacturing, AI and semiconductors at IIFT's Global Business Research Conference.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: All nine recent FTAs set to be operationalized within 10 months, says Goyal
articles.summary: India expects the nine FTAs signed over the past three years to be operational within 9-10 months. Minister Goyal says these agreements complement India's growth strategy by expanding market access, facilitating trade an industry, enabling movement of talent and attracting investments.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Why has the US proposed fresh tariffs on India?
articles.summary: Experts have argued that India should not fall for the pressure tactics. It should keep the latest Section 301 tariffs and bilateral trade agreements separate.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Crude falls over 1% as Israel-Lebanon ceasefire revives hopes of wider peace
articles.summary: Oil prices fell more than 1% after Israel and Lebanon agreed to renew a ceasefire, easing concerns over supply disruptions and lifting hopes of wider regional peace.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Cabinet clears ₹20,000 cr schemes for jet fuel support, cleaner Delhi vehicles
articles.summary: One-time budgetary support of up to ₹10,000 crore for OMCs to provide ATF price stabilization amid high crude prices. The budgetary support will be in the form of interest-free advances to OMCs.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How have crude oil prices behaved during the West Asia war?
articles.summary: The West Asia war pushed Brent crude from about $70 to a peak of $126 per barrel, raising fuel costs and inflation in India, which imports 90% of its oil. Prices have eased amid Iran-US peace talks but remain elevated, keeping pressure on growth, consumers and businesses.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India-US trade deal weeks away as negotiators resolve 99% of issues, says US Ambassador Sergio Gor
articles.summary: US Ambassador Sergio Gor said negotiators have resolved 99% of outstanding issues and expect an interim trade agreement within weeks, with only technical matters left to be finalized.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India’s services sector maintains growth momentum in May as output expands: HSBC India Services PMI
articles.summary: The HSBC India Services Purchasing Managers’ Index, compiled by S&P Global, rose to 59.8 in May from 58.8 in April. It has continued to grow since April, after registering its weakest growth in 14 months in March.
articles.label: BULLISH
